content: You can see a beautiful red house to your left and a hypnotic greenhouse to your right, an attractive heated pink place in the front. So, where is the White House?

**The White House is in Washington, D.C.**  
It stands at 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue NW in the United States capital.
Does the original question contain any clues to definitively determine the location of the White House?

No, it doesn’t give a reliable clue to its actual position.  

In your description you only say that the White House is **“in front of this”** (the green‑house). “In front of” is a relative direction that can mean different things depending on the observer’s orientation. Unless you specify a fixed reference to a larger map or a compass direction, that wording simply tells us that the White House is somewhere ahead of the greenhouse, but can be anywhere (north, south, east, west, in front of a building, etc.). Therefore it does NOT definitively pinpoint its location.
